title: epipactis leptochila subsp. peitzii
intro: Epipactis leptochila subsp. peitzii, also known as the Peitz's Helleborine, is a rare, terrestrial orchid species native to Europe. It is used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been observed in its extracts. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and fighting infections, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. Epipactis leptochila subsp. peitzii is often prepared as a tincture or infusion, which can be used in traditional herbal remedies to harness its medicinal properties.
